Afghanistan.notes: e-Visa: Visitors must arrive at Kabul International (KBL)..

Algeria.notes: Visitors can apply for a visa on arrival to travel to designated areas through travel agencies licensed by the Algerian government..

Argentina.notes: An Electronic Authorization (AVE or ETA) option is available for valid visa holders of the United States.. As of 1 October 2017, visas are issued with a 10-year validity period..

Australia.notes: May apply online (Online Visitor e600 visa)..

Austria.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Belarus.notes: For a maximum total stay of 90 days within any 1 calendar year..

Belgium.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Benin.notes: Must have an international vaccination certificate.. Three types of electronic visa are offered: the e-Visa valid for 30 days for a single entry (50 EUR), the e-Visa valid for 30 days for several (multiple) entries (75 EUR), and the e-Visa valid for 90 days to make several (multiple) entries (100 EUR)..

Bhutan.notes: The Sustainable Development Fee (SDF) of 200 USD per person, per night for almost all visitors to Bhutan. Additionally, if payment is made in US dollars from September 1, 2023 to August 31, 2027, the SDF is 100 USD..

Bolivia.notes: Visitors must complete an online application form, but still need to visit an embassy to receive a sticker visa..

Bosnia and Herzegovina.notes: 90 days within any 6-month period.. ID card valid..

Bulgaria.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Canada.notes: Visa not required for permanent residents of the United States..

Cape Verde.notes: Travelers must obtain a pre-registration (EASE) before departure and have an application number to avoild higer fees upon arrival..

Croatia.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Cuba.notes: Can be extended up to 90 days with a fee..

Czech Republic.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Denmark.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Egypt.notes: Visa on arrival availability may vary by port of entry and airline; obtaining an e-Visa prior to travel is recommended..

Estonia.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Ethiopia.notes: e-Visa holders must arrive via Addis Ababa Bole International Airport..

Finland.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

France.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Gabon.notes: e-Visa holders must arrive via Libreville International Airport..

Germany.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Ghana.notes: Pre-approved visa can be picked up on arrival..

Greece.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Hungary.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Iceland.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

India.notes: e-Visa holders must arrive via 32 designated airports or 5 designated seaports.. An Indian e-Tourist Visa may only be obtained twice within 1 calendar year.. Foreigners of Pakistani origin or who hold a Pakistani Passport are not eligible for an e-Visa. Foreigners who are not Pakistani nationals, but whose parents or grandparents (either paternal or maternal) were born in, or were permanent residents in Pakistan, are also not eligible for an e-Visa..

Iraq.notes: Visitors can also apply for an e-Visa (30 days) to visit the Iraqi Kurdistan Region..

Ireland.notes: Visa is issued free of charge.. Visa waiver for UK 'C' visa holders. Entry permitted only if first point of entry to the Common Travel Area is in the UK.. Guards not present at the Ireland-Northern Ireland..

Israel.notes: Foreign nationals entering Israel through Ben Gurion Airport are issued an entry permit upon arrival, which serves as proof of legal stay and must be retained until departure. Admission is determined solely by Israeli border and immigration authorities at the airport.. Travelers who are denied entry and issued a return order must contact an Israeli diplomatic mission for information about future travel eligibility.. Since 1 August 2024, visa-exempt travelers are required to complete the ETA-IL electronic travel authorization prior to travel and may enter Israel only after receiving approval or holding a valid visa..

Italy.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Jordan.notes: Visa can be obtained upon arrival, it will cost a total of 40 JOD, obtainable at most international ports of entry and land border crossings. (except King Hussein/Allenby Bridge ).

Kenya.notes: Applications can be submitted up to 90 days prior to travel and must be submitted at least 3 days in advance.. eTA fee is 32.50 USD.. Proof of reservation at the hotel where visitors plan to stay is required (if staying with friends, an invitation letter is also acceptable).. Yellow fever vaccination certificate is required if coming from endemic countries..

South Korea.notes: The validity period of a K-ETA is 3 years from the date of approval..

Laos.notes: 18 of the 33 border crossings are only open to regular visa holders.. e-Visa may be used to enter Laos through the Luang Prabang, Pakse and Vientiane international airports, 3 Thai-Lao Friendship Bridges, in Boten (road and railroad ), and in Vientiane (at Khamsavath railway station ).. Visa on arrival is available at the Luang Prabang, Pakse and Vientiane international airports, 4 Thai-Lao Friendship Bridges and 7 border crossings..

Latvia.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Liechtenstein.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Lithuania.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Luxembourg.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Madagascar.notes: For stays of 61 to 90 days, the visa fee is 59 USD..

Malaysia.notes: The electronic Malaysia Digital Arrival Card must be submitted within three days before the date of arrival in Malaysia..

Malta.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Mexico.notes: Exemption applies only to holders of valid multiple-entry visas or residence permits; admission remains discretionary..

Myanmar.notes: e-Visa holders must arrive via Yangon, Nay Pyi Taw or Mandalay airports or via land border crossings with Thailand — Tachileik, Myawaddy and Kawthaung or India — Rih Khaw Dar and Tamu.. e-Visa is available for tourism only..

Namibia.notes: Available at Hosea Kutako International Airport and other points of entry..

Netherlands.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

New Zealand.notes: Holders of an Australian Permanent Resident Visa or Resident Return Visa may be granted a New Zealand Resident Visa on arrival permitting indefinite stay (pursuant to the Trans-Tasman Travel Arrangement ), subject to meeting character requirements and obtaining an Electronic Travel Authority prior to departure..

Nicaragua.notes: Nationals of Albania with a visa issued by Canada, the USA or a Schengen Member State can obtain a visa upon arrival for a maximum of 30 days..

North Macedonia.notes: ID card valid.. 90 days within any 180 day period..

Norway.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Oman.notes: As of January 2026, Albanians belong to the first group of visa exemptions in Oman..

Panama.notes: Passengers holding a multiple entry visa issued by Australia, Canada, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, the US, the UK or an EU member state are exempt from the visa..

Papua New Guinea.notes: Visitors may apply for a visa online under the "Tourist - Own Itinerary" category..

Paraguay.notes: Visitors must complete an online application form, but still need to visit an embassy to receive a sticker visa..

Philippines.notes: Tourist visas must be obtained in advance through a Philippine diplomatic mission; limited online pre-application systems do not constitute visa-free or eVisa entry.. Residents of the United Arab Emirates may obtain an e-Visa through the official Philippine eVisa website. A valid Emirati residence visa must be shown upon an eVisa application..

Poland.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Portugal.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Qatar.notes: Travelers can apply for a visa on the Hayya website..

Romania.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Serbia.notes: ID card valid.. 90 days within any 6-month period..

Seychelles.notes: Application can be submitted up to 30 days before travel.. Visitors must upload a reservation confirmation(s) for each visitor's location of stay in Seychelles.. Yellow fever vaccination certificate is required if coming from endemic countries.. Payment of the fee (EUR 10) by credit or debit card.. Valid for one journey only and it expires once exit the country..

Slovakia.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Slovenia.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

South Sudan.notes: Obtainable online.. Printed visa authorization must be presented at the time of travel..

Spain.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Suriname.notes: An entrance fee of USD 50 or EUR 50 must be paid online prior to arrival.. Multiple entry e-Visa is also available..

Sweden.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Switzerland.notes: 90 days within any 180 day period in the Schengen Area..

Syria.notes: Entry is subject to additional security clearance; airline acceptance and port-of-entry conditions may vary..

Tajikistan.notes: Citizens aged 55 and above can travel for 14 days without a visa..

Turkmenistan.notes: When transiting between two non-bordering countries, visitors can obtain a Turkmenistan transit visa for a five-day stay. This must be applied for in advance at the Turkmenistan Embassy. Visitors must also submit copies of the visas for the country of entry into Turkmenistan and the country of departure from Turkmenistan. Visa fee is 20 USD..

United Kingdom.notes: Albanian citizens who hold a valid entry visa for Australia, Canada, New Zealand or the United States, and a valid airline ticket for travel via the United Kingdom, as part of a journey to or from one of those countries and/or are permanent residents of the European Union, Australia, Canada and New Zealand do not require a Direct Airside Transit visa..

United States.notes: May not apply for an immigrant visa..

Uzbekistan.notes: 5-day visa-free transit at the international airports if holding a confirmed onward ticket for a flight to a third country..

Venezuela.notes: Introduction of Electronic Visa System for Tourist and Business Travelers..

Vietnam.notes: e-Visa is valid for 90 days and multiple entry.. Phú Quốc visa exemption for up to 30 days..

Yemen.notes: Yemen introduced an e-Visa system for visitors who meet certain eligibility requirements (group travel of 10 or more people, business trips, and transit etc.)..

Abkhazia.stay: Tourists from all countries (except Georgia) can visit Abkhazia for a period not exceeding 24 hours as part of an organized tourist group.

Mount Athos.stay: Special permit required (4 days: €25 for Eastern Orthodox Church visitors, €35 for non-Orthodox visitors, €18 for students). There is a visitors' quota: maximum 100 Eastern Orthodox Church and 10 non-Orthodox per day and women are not allowed.

Crimea.stay: Visa policy of Russia is applied.

UN Buffer Zone in Cyprus.stay: Access Permit is required for travelling inside the zone, except Civil Use Areas.

Jan Mayen.stay: Permit issued by the local police required for staying for less than 24 hours and permit issued by the Norwegian police for staying for more than 24 hours.

South Ossetia.stay: To enter South Ossetia, visitors must have a multiple-entry visa for Russia and register their stay with the Migration Service of the Ministry of Internal Affairs within 3 days.

Svalbard.stay: Unlimited stay.. Everybody may live and work in Svalbard indefinitely regardless of country of citizenship..

Eritrea outside Asmara.stay: To travel in the rest of the country, a Travel Permit for Foreigners is required (20 Eritrean nakfa ).

Tristan da Cunha.stay: Permission to land required for 15/30 pounds sterling (yacht/ship passenger) for Tristan da Cunha Island or 20 pounds sterling for Gough Island, Inaccessible Island or Nightingale Islands.

India PAP/RAP.stay: Protected Area Permit (PAP) required for whole states of Nagaland and Sikkim and parts of states Manipur, Arunachal Pradesh, Uttaranchal, Jammu and Kashmir, Rajasthan, Himachal Pradesh. Restricted Area Permit (RAP) required for all of Andaman and Nicobar Islands and parts of Sikkim. Some of these requirements are occasionally lifted for a year.

Kish Island.stay: Visitors to Kish Island do not require a visa.

North Korea outside Pyongyang.stay: People are not allowed to leave the capital city, tourists can only leave the capital with a governmental tourist guide (no independent moving)

Palestine.stay: Arrival by sea to Gaza Strip not allowed.

Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Province.stay: OIVR permit required (15+5 Tajikistani Somoni ) and another special permit (free of charge) is required for Lake Sarez.

Tibet Autonomous Region.stay: Tibet Travel Permit required (10 US Dollars ).

French West Indies.stay: 90 days; French West Indies refers to Martinique, Guadeloupe, Saint Martin and Saint Barthélemy.

Pitcairn Islands.stay: 14 days visa free and landing fee US$35 or tax of US$5 if not going ashore.

United States Minor Outlying Islands.stay: Special permits required for Baker Island, Howland Island, Jarvis Island, Johnston Atoll, Kingman Reef, Midway Atoll, Palmyra Atoll and Wake Island.

South Georgia and the South Sandwich Isl.stay: Pre-arrival permit from the Commissioner required (72 hours/1 month for 110/160 pounds sterling ).
